channel.title: GitHub Copilot on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in GitHub Copilot on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: 🎨 Generate with GPT-image-2
item.description:
The new create-image skill brings GPT-image-2 generation into the developer workflow. Instead of opening a browser, copying a prompt into a design tool, and manually moving files around, you can generate visuals from your terminal or agent workflow using Azure OpenAI.

It is a custom skill I created in the sujithq/skills repository, so the goal is practical automation rather than a generic image-generation tutorial.

That makes it useful for blog covers, documentation diagrams, UI placeholders, presentation visuals, and quick concept exploration. The important bit is that the skill is designed for developer environments: it uses Azure OpenAI, supports keyless Azure RBAC authentication, and saves the generated output into your project.

item.title: 🔑 Easier BYOK for GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: GitHub Copilot CLI supports Bring Your Own Key (BYOK), letting you point the CLI at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, Azure OpenAI, or Anthropic instead of the default Copilot service. The capability is powerful, but the raw workflow requires you to export several environment variables every time you switch providers. This post walks through the manual approach first, shows why it becomes cumbersome, and then introduces gh-copilot-byok, a .NET global tool that wraps the whole flow with a polished interactive interface.
